changedFiles.reason: Defines the approved SBOM-chain repair scope, sources, acceptance, and verification requirements.
Records generated evidence changes, review boundaries, and verification results.
Existing Dependabot change updates ruby/setup-ruby from 1.317.0 to 1.318.0 and drives the generated evidence change.
Generated SBOM now records the pinned ruby/setup-ruby 1.318.0 commit.
Generated provenance now records the digest of the refreshed SBOM.
Generated release evidence now records the refreshed SBOM and provenance digests.
Release preparation metadata now records the exact byte-level digests of the refreshed SBOM and provenance.
Generated Cockpit status for the active repair Work Item.
Generated archive discovery index updated when the Work Item is archived.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Generate committed status or supply-chain evidence with repository commands; never hand-edit generated files.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: SBOM, provenance, and release digests were produced by scripts/check_supply_chain.py with --write and then validated without --write.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep the repair limited to evidence derived from the existing Dependabot workflow update.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The product diff contains only the existing compatibility.yml bump and its three deterministic supply-chain evidence files.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not change dependency locks, generators, tests, runtime behavior, or unrelated workflow configuration.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: No scripts, tests, locks, runtime files, or additional workflow lines changed.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not bypass failing or pending GitHub branch-protection checks.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: PR #39 will be merged only after the updated branch reports all required checks passed.

risk.detail: The repair is deterministic generated evidence for one pinned Action update; full repository and PR checks still gate merge.

observedIssues: Dependabot updated the workflow Action pin without refreshing committed SBOM evidence, so smoke and every Python compatibility lane failed.
The SBOM digest is transitively embedded in provenance and release-digests, requiring the complete generated evidence chain to be refreshed.
The full quality gate showed release.json also stores byte-level SBOM and provenance digests, so those two derived metadata fields required synchronization.

reviewReadiness.reason: All required verification passed and no residual risk remains.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm the workflow diff remains the original ruby/setup-ruby pin update.
Confirm SBOM changes only the ruby/setup-ruby component pin and provenance/release files change only derived digests.
Confirm release.json changes only its SBOM and provenance digest fields.
Confirm all GitHub checks pass before merge.

userCorrectionsCaptured: The user approved the focused PR #39 SBOM repair and requested merge followed by cleanup.
After completion, provide explicit development instructions.
userCorrectionSolidification: The repair uses the repository generator and includes the full deterministic SBOM-to-provenance-to-release-digest chain.
Merge and cleanup remain conditional on passing required GitHub checks.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Reconfirmed the expanded evidence chain, including release metadata digest synchronization, after the full quality gate exposed the transitive release invariant.
Confirmed deterministic generated diffs, synchronized release metadata, passing supply-chain validators and both targeted regression tests, and exact scope before rerunning the full finish gate.

knownGaps: No generator, dependency lock, test, runtime, unrelated release claim, or unrelated workflow behavior is changed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The workflow Action inventory changes while committed supply-chain evidence remains stale.
evidence: Original PR #39 GitHub Actions failure logs

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The generated repair changes unrelated evidence or implementation files.

intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: The complete deterministic evidence chain and its release metadata references now match the pinned workflow Action without changing generator or runtime behavior.

overclaimPrevention: Do not report PR #39 merged or cleaned until all GitHub checks pass and lifecycle closure succeeds.
